case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on the provided surface.

plan.reasoning:
SMILES Consistency Check: The SMILES [N]=[NH] represents NNH (diazene radical / NNH species). Atom index 0 is N with SP2 hybridization, degree 1, and 1 radical electron. Atom index 1 is N with SP2 hybridization, degree 1, and 0 radical electrons. This is a reactive species (radical on N at index 0).

Adsorbate Analysis: NNH has a terminal N radical (index 0) and a NH terminus (index 1). The most chemically reasonable adsorption mode is through the radical N (index 0) binding to the surface, as it has an unpaired electron. This is a ReactiveSpecies.

Surface Analysis: The Cu3Ag(111) surface has available sites: Ontop (Ag, Cu), Bridge (Ag-Cu, Cu-Cu), Hollow-3 (Ag-Cu-Cu, Cu-Cu-Cu), Hollow-4 (Ag-Cu-Cu-Cu). For NNH, the most common and energetically favorable adsorption mode on transition metal surfaces is end-on through the terminal N radical at an ontop site. Cu ontop is the most abundant and typically most reactive site on Cu3Ag alloy surfaces.

Plan: Use ReactiveSpecies with the radical N (index 0) binding ontop a Cu atom. This is the most physically motivated starting configuration for NNH on a Cu-rich surface. Only 1 attempt is allowed, so I choose the most likely lowest-energy configuration: N-end (radical end, index 0) ontop Cu.
